All right, so now we are diving into sales manager. Okay, so the sales manager is all about acquisition and owning the entire acquisition process. From doing calls if they needed to call to confirm appointments, to monitoring lead nurture if you're using automated lead nurture or something like that. So they're getting back to everybody that has any questions to setting up the orientation room if they need to set things up and make sure that everybody is situated if they're having to fill out pre-questionnaires, jumping on the scale before the orientation, they need to own the entire process. And they're also responsible for selling all of your leads into your challenge, into your program. So really understanding, you know, objection overcomes, really understanding the gym launch principles to the three pillar pitch and what your program all includes in how it's gonna operate and how it's gonna work. So they need to own the entire acquisition process and they need to really be proactive instead of reactive when it comes to sales because sometimes people don't show up for appointments. So it's really important that the sales manager understands that and works leads and is a hustler, right? So in addition to that, you could have them doing things like halfway meetings, abandoned hope meetings where they are converting people that they sold into the challenge and keeping them as 12 month members. Now I've seen it done both ways where a challenge manager might do that because they've built a rapport with them. Sometimes the owner sits down because the owner's there all day long and has a good rapport. Sometimes if the challenge manager isn't that great at sales and the owner is not necessarily at the gym every day, the sales manager is a perfect person to do it. So whoever you feel most comfortable having that halfway meeting, I think the members would understand, regardless of who they meet with, you can have them selling at the end during wayouts and maybe converting them to a year of change or things like that. But the main point is that the person that you bring on for your sales manager, they really need to own the entire acquisition process, converting 15% of leads. And that has to do with nurture, sales, follow-up, follow-through, handwritten cards after the sales been done. So the person is really thankful for the time that they spent coming to the gym and sitting down and listening to the program that we have to offer. So when it comes time for the owner to step away from sales because they're really working on scaling their business, working on lead gen, working on the different things that they need to to improve every aspect of their business. So their focus might not just lie on sales at this point, but how do we make this area better? How do we acquire? How do we teach some of these people to be leaders? How do we do all of this stuff? That all takes a lot of time. And the easiest way to get that time is to replace themselves in the sales process with somebody else that's really good at sales. So you could hire two part-time sales managers at first, whether you give them 10 hours a week each or 15 hours or 20 hours a week each. It really depends on how many leads you're bringing in. That will kind of dictate how many hours you give them. But with the correct base, maybe it's $15 an hour plus commission or $10 an hour plus commission, if they're really good at selling, they can still make a great wage from closing deals. But you also have two people to choose from in case one of them isn't that good. It can be a little bit scary to step out of the sales room for the first time as an owner who's been selling for years and then delegate that to somebody else. So having two people there, it usually softens the burden on the one person to perform as good as the owner was, which is really difficult to do. So how to hire this person is in the ownership training course and how to train them is going to be in this portal. There is a downloadable share funnel that you can make your own. So replace all of the videos that are in there with your own and how this person is going to operate at your gym and what a day in the life of a sales manager looks like at your gym. The four hours document is in here as well. And then this sheet that is a summary of their duties and everything that they would be doing is also downloadable. So this is a really fun position when it comes to finding a sales manager, it's one of the more difficult positions to find because a lot of people don't have experience selling exactly what we do. But once you find them and they start closing after you train them really good, it is a breath of fresh air because the owner really does have the freedom to start working on the business, which is a lot of fun.
